close_notes:
(All Timestamps must be in UTC)
Issue Summary: wdc2-qz1-sr3-rk218-s14 in mzone767 is in NotReady state
SME Findings: sme-team: wdc2-qz1-sr3-rk218-s14 in mzone767 is in NotReady state
Reason of Escalation:
Resolution Steps:
The CORE team investigated incident NNR and discovered that the "deadbeef" label was missing. CORE collaborated with the Compute team to add the label and manually triggered CFN, which successfully removed the node from the cluster.
Signature of Issue:
Jira/Github/Other Tickets:
Category/Subcategory:
Timeline Summary:
 CORE team received a "Node Not Ready" alert in the core-monitoring Slack channel.
* Node removal failed due to a missing "deadbeef" timestamp.
* Investigation found the node was in a "not ready" state, but was accessible and had running pods.
* Attempts to fix the issue by stopping and starting the kubelet service were unsuccessful.
* The node was found to be hosting 17 internal customer VSIs.
* CORE tainted the node 
* The CORE team engaged the CSRE team for assistance.
* Joint efforts by CORE and CSRE teams to restart frr and kubelet services failed to resolve the issue.
* We checked the status of iobricks and fabcon services, and both services were running.
* The decision was made to remove the node from the cluster by adding a "deadbeef" label and triggering CFN.
* As per fleets team discussion with CSRE team for similar issue it was said that it was upto the operational team decision to remove the node and since all the VSI belonged to internal customer we did not make this as CIE 
Resolution Runbook: Not Needed / Not Available / URL
